notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.subtitle: The page you are looking for does not exist.

common.error: An error occurred while loading data.

ffdc.title: FinnFoundDaChest
ffdc.description: Solo 2D adventure game in plain Java (Adventure Time universe).
ffdc.long_description:
First Java project on my own. Solo speedrun game: Finn explores a fixed map, finds keys, opens doors, reaches the final chest.

Plain Java, no engine. Finn's sprites drawn by hand. WASD movement, collisions, item handling, debug mode (FPS, latency, coords), toggleable music, HTML README generated with Pandoc.

agile.title: Agile Welcome Project
agile.description: Java Rogue-Like playable in the terminal. 10 Scrum sprints, team of 4.
agile.long_description:
Agile Welcome Project (BUT2). Terminal-playable Rogue-Like in Java, 4 people, 10 weekly Scrum sprints on GitLab.

Multiple classes (warrior, mage…), procedural floors, chests, equipment, final boss, admin mode with cheat codes. Spec, issues, retro and playable increment per sprint.

sae33.title: SAE 3.3 Project
sae33.description: JavaFX k-NN classification app on a dataset. MVC, team of 4.
sae33.long_description:
SAE 3.3 (BUT2), team of 4 ("Mathieu Fanclub"). JavaFX app for k-NN visualization and classification.

Loads a dataset (Iris), plots a 2D scatter with chosen axes, adds an unknown point and classifies it via k-NN. Multi-window drag-and-drop, generic CSV reader.

My part: mockups, jalon 1 prototypes, coloring and classification. Strict MVC (FXML, SceneBuilder), JUnit tests, full UML.

mte.title: MineToEarn
mte.description: Minecraft plugin rewarding mining + web interface. Team project.
mte.long_description: Minecraft plugin (Spigot) that turns mining into usable currency. Web interface to track progress and run transactions. Team project.

corpsamer.title: Nuit de l'Info: CorpsAMer
corpsamer.description: HTML/CSS/JS webapp for Nuit de l'Info hackathon. Team project.
corpsamer.long_description: Webapp built during Nuit de l'Info (one-night contest). Vanilla HTML/CSS/JS, team of several students, topic imposed by organizers.

talkul.title: Talkul
talkul.description: Java EE real-time messaging webapp. Tomcat + Postgres.
talkul.long_description:
Web messaging app in Java EE (Servlets, JSP, Tomcat) with Postgres, for the server-side web dev module.

Auth, conversations, message sending and refresh. Externalized config (`config.prop`) so credentials are never hardcoded. Jackson for JSON/XML, Jakarta Migration for recent Servlet versions.

woze.title: Woze
woze.description: Multi-modal itinerary calculator (train/bus/plane) in JavaFX. Team of 3.
woze.long_description:
SAE 2.01-2.02 (BUT1), team of 3. Java/JavaFX app computing multi-modal itineraries between cities, optimizing for price, duration or CO2e.

3 successive versions: (1) shortest path single-mode on a graph from CSV, (2) multi-modal with transfer costs, (3) relative weighting of criteria + trip history. MVC, FXML controllers, PMD checks.

My part: OOP (Voyage/Place/Platform, CSV import, exceptions, filtering) and graph (edge weighting, transfers).

maio.title: Ma.io
maio.description: Multiplayer .io-like game (mayonnaise vs aliens) in Node.js + JS. Team project.
maio.long_description:
JavaScript SAE: multiplayer web game (.io-like) on the theme mayonnaise vs aliens.

Separate client/server. Node.js server (ES modules) owns shared state (player positions, mayo stains, leaderboard). JS client bundled via Webpack. WebSockets for real-time.

Key wins: refactor to cleanly split client and server after a monolithic start, TDD on the Player class, collision tuning through iteration, bots that don't just wander randomly.

magare.title: MaGare
magare.description: Web reproduction of SNCF's Ma Gare app with real-time API.
magare.long_description:
Web reproduction of SNCF's Ma Gare mobile app, deployed on GitHub Pages. Personal project, built solo end-to-end.

React 19, TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind, DaisyUI, React Router. SNCF Navitia API for real-time departures/arrivals and trip detail. Side-by-side board (desktop) or toggle (mobile), swipe sheet for train detail, destination filter with favorites, default station in localStorage, light/dark themes in official SNCF colors.

weathersport.title: WeatherSport
weathersport.description: Weather + sports recommendations based on conditions. Spring Boot + Vue.js. Nextoo internship.
weathersport.long_description:
First project of the Nextoo internship (Nextoo Academy). Matches weather data for a city with a sports catalog to recommend fitting activities (rain → indoor, good weather → outdoor).

Spring Boot backend with n-tier architecture (DTO/BO/DAO), REST API, JPA/Hibernate, Postgres in Docker, JUnit/Mockito tests. Vue.js 3 + Vuetify front. Docker Compose, GitLab CI/CD + SonarQube/JACOCO.

nextmovie.title: NextMovie
nextmovie.description: Movie recommendation engine. Spring Boot + Vue.js. Nextoo internship.
nextmovie.long_description:
Second project of the Nextoo internship, built solo in 2 weeks (skills validation). Recommends films based on watched films and user ratings.

Spring Boot with TheMovieDB API, global ExceptionHandler, n-tier architecture. Front Vue.js 3 / Vuetify, 5 views (home, film detail, profile, recommendations, top rated).

gds.title: GDS
gds.description: Internal stock management tool. Spring Boot + Vue.js. Nextoo internship.
gds.long_description:
Third project of the Nextoo internship: internal stock management (IT gear, phones, vehicles). Joined an existing project team directly.

Spring Boot + Vue.js + Postgres + Docker. Full CRUD, server-side filtering / sorting / pagination, fast-entry forms. Daily standups, code reviews on merge requests, owned a User Story from day one.

dinnermatch.title: DinnerMatch
dinnermatch.description: Platform to share themed dinners between hotel guests. Spring Boot + React.
dinnermatch.long_description:
Connects hotel guests who want to share themed dinners based on shared interests. Startup Week project (BUT3), team of 5, deployed at dinnermatch.fr.

Front: React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind 4, DaisyUI 5, theme editor for hoteliers, responsive calendar, role-based dashboards (Client / Hotelier / Admin). Back: Spring Boot 4, Java 21, Spring Security + JWT, JPA/Hibernate, Postgres 17, email via Scaleway TEM. GitLab CI/CD, Docker, Nginx reverse proxy.

My part: backend (API, JWT, matching, admin CRUD panels).

refugegourmand.title: Le Refuge Gourmand
refugegourmand.description: Room booking website for a fictional restaurant. Next.js, Prisma, NextAuth, Stripe.
refugegourmand.long_description:
Academic entrepreneurship project: room booking website for a fictional restaurant. Next.js static export on GitHub Pages.

NextAuth + bcrypt, Stripe payments, Prisma (Postgres), React Hook Form + Zod validation, Tailwind. Atomic Design.

weerdlebot.title: WeerdleBot
weerdlebot.description: Node.js bot that auto-plays Weerdle (Pokémon Wordle) with a control dashboard.
weerdlebot.long_description:
Personal bot for Weerdle (Pokémon variant of Wordle on pokengine.com). Sends authenticated HTTP requests, analyzes responses, adapts strategy.

Web dashboard (Node.js + HTML/CSS/JS) for start/stop, live logs and NordVPN rotation to avoid IP bans. Runs in Docker.

snackandcatch.title: Snack & Catch
snackandcatch.description: Web app for Cobblemon (Pokémon in Minecraft). Recipes, spawn spots, addons. Next.js + Drizzle.
snackandcatch.long_description:
Companion app for the Cobblemon mod. Centralizes recipes, spawn spots and addons scattered across community wikis.

Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind, Drizzle on Postgres (Neon), deployed on Vercel. Ingestion pipeline that scrapes the wikis and structures the data.

numismatic.title: Numismatic Reimagined (WIP)
numismatic.description: Multi-loader Minecraft currency mod (Fabric + Forge), MC 1.20.1. WIP.
numismatic.long_description:
Currency system for Minecraft inspired by Numismatic Overhaul, rewritten to run on Fabric and Forge in parallel.

Java 17, Architectury API, multi-module Gradle (common / fabric / forge). Not yet released.

ghastrider.title: GhastRider (WIP)
ghastrider.description: Minecraft NeoForge mod: dash for the Happy Ghast (MC 1.21+). WIP.
ghastrider.long_description:
Adds a dash mechanic to the Happy Ghast (MC 1.21). Player consumes ice blocks to propel themselves.

Java 21, NeoForge, TOML config to tune cooldown / strength / cost without recompiling. Not yet released.

profile.hero: Full-stack developer apprentice at Mandarine Academy, 3rd year Computer Science (BUT) at IUT de Lille. I enjoy team projects as much as solo ones. Have a look around.
profile.about:
I'm **Gaspard Catry**, 20, full-stack developer based in **Lille**. 3rd year **BUT Computer Science** at **IUT de Lille** (ranked 12/72 in S5, GPA 14.81/20), apprenticeship at **Mandarine Academy** on a **PHP / Symfony / Tailwind** stack.

Before that, internship at **Nextoo** (Roubaix): intensive **Java 21 / Spring Boot 3.4 / Vue.js 3 / Docker** training, 3 internal web apps. Written recommendation from the Deputy MD at the end.

On the side I run personal projects in production (**MaGare**, web reproduction of the SNCF app) and dabble in open-source on weekends. Goal: software engineer on full-stack web apps.
profile.positioning:
I'm finishing a **BUT Computer Science** (Application Development track), with experience combining **Java / Spring Boot / Vue.js** (Nextoo internship) and **PHP / Symfony / Tailwind** (Mandarine apprenticeship).

Looking for a **full-stack developer** or **junior software engineer** role in a demanding technical team where I can work on architecture and code quality.
profile.reflexive:
I learn fast in a demanding technical environment. Ramping up on Spring Boot and Vue in 10 weeks at Nextoo showed me I can absorb a new stack quickly. Working on a live production app at Mandarine made me grasp the real impact of every commit.

I'm more effective when I understand *why*, not just *how*. My biggest growth moments came when my mentor took the time to explain an architectural choice.

Moving into a company also taught me to manage my time differently: estimate, prioritize, communicate on progress.
profile.strengths:
**Fast learning.** Confirmed by 3 full projects in 10 weeks on an unknown stack at Nextoo.

**Technical rigor.** Tests, docs, conventions (GitFlow, conventional commits, clean architecture). Positive code reviews.

**Curiosity.** Personal projects (DinnerMatch, MaGare, this portfolio), open-source, regular tech watch.

**Versatility.** Java/Spring, PHP/Symfony, React/TS, Vue.js — quick adaptation to whatever stack is in place.
profile.improvements:
**Meeting communication.** Reserved at first. Improving via daily stand-ups at Mandarine, but still an area to work on.

**Time estimation.** Tendency to underestimate (especially front). Learning to break things down finer and leave realistic buffers.

**Asking for help.** I can dive into a technical problem too long before reaching out. Trying to spot earlier when a colleague would help me move faster.

**Stress.** First weeks in a company were intense. Better organized now, but tight deadlines remain a point of attention.
profile.aboutVision:
What stays irreplaceable in a developer: understanding a business need and turning it into a solution, architecting a system, owning the technical responsibility, handling team and client relationships. AI is a tool I use daily — an amplifier, not a replacement.

Goal: software engineer on full-stack web apps.

ghastrider.name: GhastRider
ghastrider.tagline: Dash mechanic for the Happy Ghast

overview.title: Overview
overview.content:
GhastRider adds a charge-and-dash mechanic to the Happy Ghast mount, inspired by the horse jump bar system.

Hold the dash key to charge, release to boost forward with a satisfying wind sound. Feed ice to your ghast for speed boosts!

Features

  • Charge bar — replaces the XP bar when riding a Happy Ghast
  • Minimum threshold — quick taps are ignored (2/18 bars)
  • Overcharge decay — holding too long reduces charge
  • Proportional cooldown — shorter charges mean shorter cooldowns
  • Auto-recharge — keep holding G through cooldown
  • Vertical boost — maintains altitude during dash
  • Ice feeding — feed ice for speed effects
  • Custom sounds — wind whoosh, ice crunch, happy purr

dash.title: Dash Mechanic
dash.content:
How to Dash

  1. Mount a Happy Ghast (equip it with a harness)
  2. Hold G to charge — the bar fills up
  3. Release G at the right moment for maximum power
  4. The bar shows the cooldown shrinking
  5. Once empty, dash again — or keep holding G to auto-recharge

Parameters

ParameterValue
Minimum charge2/18 bars (~11%)
Max charge time2 seconds
Max cooldown3 seconds
Vertical boost30%
KeyG (configurable)

ice.title: Ice Feeding
ice.content:
Feed ice to your Happy Ghast by right-clicking for temporary speed boosts.

ItemEffectDurationNotes
IceSpeed I10sDuration stacks
Packed IceSpeed II10sDuration stacks
Blue IceSpeed III10sDuration stacks
Powder Snow BucketSpeed I10sEmpties to bucket

Feeding a different ice type resets the duration to 10s with the new speed level.

Effects

  • Ice crunch sound
  • Happy ghast purr sound
  • Item particles of the block you fed

installation.title: Installation
installation.content:
Requirements

  • Minecraft 26.1.2
  • Fabric Loader or NeoForge
  • Fabric API (Fabric only)

Steps

  1. Install Fabric Loader or NeoForge for MC 26.1.2
  2. For Fabric: also install Fabric API
  3. Download the JAR from GitHub Releases
  4. Place the JAR in your mods/ folder
  5. Launch the game

commands.title: Commands
commands.content:
Debug Function

/function ghastrider:debug

Sets up a test environment:

  • Peaceful difficulty
  • Disables time, mob spawning, weather
  • Day + clear weather
  • Clears your inventory
  • Kills existing Happy Ghasts
  • Spawns 3 invulnerable Happy Ghasts
  • Gives: harness, all ice types, snowballs

Requires a Creative world with cheats enabled (Superflat recommended).

changelog.title: Changelog
changelog.content:
v26.1.2.1-alpha.1

April 16, 2026

First public release:

  • Dash mechanic with charge bar
  • Overcharge decay with minimum threshold
  • Proportional cooldown
  • Auto-recharge when holding G
  • Vertical boost during dash
  • Ice feeding with Speed effects
  • Custom sounds (wind, crunch, purr)
  • Item particles
  • Debug function
  • Fabric and NeoForge support for MC 26.1.2

overview.title: Overview
overview.content:
Numismatic Reimagined is a Minecraft currency mod inspired by Numismatic Overhaul, rebuilt from scratch.

It adds a full economy system with multiple coin denominations, shops, piggy banks, and villager trade integration.

This mod is currently in early development. Phase 1 (coin items) is complete.

currency.title: Currency System
currency.content:
Five denominations with a base-100 exchange rate:

CoinValueExchange
Bronze1Base unit
Silver100= 100 Bronze
Gold10,000= 100 Silver
Netherite1,000,000= 100 Gold
Star CoinUniqueAchievement reward

The Star Coin is a trophy earned by accumulating 1,000 Netherite Coins worth of transactions. Unique per player but not soulbound.

roadmap.title: Roadmap
roadmap.content:
The mod is being developed in 12 phases:

  1. Coin Items — Complete
  2. Purse System
  3. Piggy Bank Block
  4. Shop Block
  5. Configuration System
  6. Loot Tables + Mob Drops
  7. Villager Trades
  8. Marketplace + Commands
  9. Achievements + Star Coin
  10. Security Hardening
  11. Addon API
  12. NeoForge + MC 1.21.1 Port

overview.intro: GhastRider turns the Happy Ghast into a real aerial combat mount. Charge your dash and blast through the sky, feed your ghast ice to go even faster.
overview.screenshotRiding: Screenshot: flying on a Happy Ghast
overview.chargeBar: Charge bar — replaces the XP bar when riding a Happy Ghast
overview.minThreshold: Minimum threshold — quick taps are ignored (2/18 bars)
overview.overchargeDecay: Overcharge decay — holding too long reduces charge
overview.proportionalCd: Proportional cooldown — shorter charges mean shorter cooldowns
overview.autoRecharge: Auto-recharge — keep holding G through cooldown to recharge
overview.iceFeeding: Ice feeding — feed ice for speed effects
overview.customSounds: Custom sounds — wind whoosh on dash, ice crunch on feeding
overview.particles: Particles — item particles of the block you fed
overview.tip: Release at 80%+ charge for the best boost. But don't hold too long — charge decays after max!
overview.dashDesc: Hold G to charge, release to boost through the air. The longer you charge, the more powerful the boost.
overview.iceDesc: Feed ice to your ghast for temporary Speed effects. Blue Ice = Speed III!
overview.qs1: Equip a Happy Ghast with a harness and mount it
overview.qs2: You'll see the charge bar replacing the XP bar
overview.compatNote: Compatible with Fabric and NeoForge for Minecraft 26.1.2

dash.intro: When you ride a Happy Ghast, the XP bar is replaced by a charge bar. That's your dash.
dash.step1: Mount a Happy Ghast equipped with a harness
dash.step4: Wait for the cooldown (the bar empties) then go again
dash.overchargeTip: Overcharge starts when the bar is full. Charge slowly drops to the minimum threshold (2/18). Release before to keep more power!
dash.tipRelease: Release when the bar is almost full for the best boost. No need to wait for 100%.
dash.tipAutoRecharge: Keep holding G even during cooldown — the dash will auto-recharge as soon as possible.
dash.tipOvercharge: Warning: if you hold too long after the bar is full, the charge drops back down. Release at the right moment!

ice.intro: Feed ice to your Happy Ghast (right-click while riding) to get temporary speed boosts.
ice.resetWarning: Feeding a different ice type resets the duration to 10s with the new speed level. For example, if you feed Blue Ice (Speed III) then regular Ice (Speed I), you lose Speed III.
ice.screenshotFeeding: Screenshot: feeding ice to a Happy Ghast
ice.itemParticles: Item particles of the ice block fed
ice.howToDesc: Hold an ice block and right-click your Happy Ghast while riding it. The speed effect lasts 10 seconds and can be stacked by feeding the same ice type multiple times.

install.step1: Install Fabric Loader or NeoForge for MC 26.1.2
install.step2: For Fabric: also install Fabric API
install.step3: Download the JAR from GitHub Releases

commands.debugDesc: Runs a command that sets up a test world to try the mod quickly:
commands.requiresCreative: Requires a Creative world with cheats enabled (Superflat recommended).

items.loaderSupport: Fabric and NeoForge support for MC 26.1.2

config.intro: GhastRider is configurable through Minecraft's options. Here's what you can change.
config.keybindDesc: By default, the dash key is G. You can change it in the game options:
config.keybindStep2: Go to Options > Controls > Key Binds
config.keybindStep3: Look for "GhastRider Dash" in the GhastRider category
config.keybindStep4: Click on it and press your new desired key
config.screenshotKeybind: Screenshot: controls menu showing the GhastRider Dash keybind
config.settDashKeyDesc: The key to charge and activate the dash
config.moreSettingsNote: More configuration options (charge time, cooldown, etc.) will be added in a future update.
